title: Character Info ({key:ga}, {key:g8}, {key:g} {key:Ctrl-G})
subtitle: What is this character? Where am I?
summary: ga shows the codepoint of the character under cursor (decimal/hex/octal). g8 shows the UTF-8 byte sequence. g Ctrl-G gives a verbose location report (line/col/word count/byte position).

blocks.text: Three diagnostic commands tell you what's actually in the file at the cursor — useful when something *looks* normal but isn't.

rows: Codepoint of char under cursor (Dec, Hex, Oct)
UTF-8 byte sequence of char under cursor
Brief: filename, modified flag, position percentage
Verbose: word/char/byte counts and cursor position

blocks.title: When These Save You
blocks.text:
**Invisible characters.** Code that looks fine but breaks parsing? {key:g}{key:a} on the suspicious spot reveals zero-width spaces, BOMs, non-breaking spaces, smart quotes from copy-paste.

**Encoding bugs.** A character displays as `?` or a box? {key:g}{key:8} shows the actual UTF-8 bytes. If they're wrong, your file's encoding may not match `'fileencoding'`.

**Word counts.** Need to know how long a section is? Visual-select it, then {key:g}{key:Ctrl-G} reports words/chars/bytes for the selection. Same key in Normal mode reports for the whole file.
{key:Ctrl-G} (no g) is the everyday "where am I" — show filename and position percentage. Quick and useful.
